comments.author: @davidb2206
comments.text: They have Europeans to thank for many of their best foods.  Pineapple, for example, is native to South America.  The first clear record of pineapple cultivation in Fiji dates to 1863. A farmer named Swanston grew pineapples imported from Melbourne, Australia, in his garden.  It was European trade that brought these things to Fiji, not some random seeds floating on the ocean or some bird droppings.

replies.text: Santosh Kumar it's bcz more then 90% of busines owners are indo Fijians and they are the ones developing and maintaining Fiji's roads and infrastructure, the over whelming majority of itaukies or native Fijians are double in numbers but are very relaxed and  layed back people people where as indo Fijians are hard working and always  looking for opportunities how to improve their lives for the future generations to come. The reality of itaukies living standards remains the same right from the very start of the Whitman set foot in fiji, The Whitman realised after couple years that itaukies drinks kava all night and sleep whole day with hangover therefore  the British brought in Indians to do the job and in very in very short time the fruits of their hard worked wonders and the Whitman was happy to see that their hard work was yielding good fortune too. Fiji is built by indo Fijians and the continue working for the betterment of all Fijians, I think the narrator forgot to give credit to Indian s or he did that on purpose, idk...but this is the reality of Fiji 🇫🇯

replies.author: @davidb2206
replies.text: And under the surface?   Fiji experiences highly disproportionate levels of sexual violence, with child abuse and statutory rape making up the vast majority of reported crimes.Human rights organizations have frequently highlighted that Fiji struggles with some of the highest rates of domestic and sexual violence in the world.  Up to 60% to 70% of reported rape and sexual assault victims are children under the age of 18. There have even been documented cases of infants and toddlers being assaulted, according to a report from the Australian Broadcasting Corporation.

replies.text: What a beautiful perspective. The line “love is free” says so much about what many people around the world seem to be missing today. Do you think younger generations in Fiji still hold on to that mindset, or is modern life starting to change it? 🌴✨

comments.author: @thebroadlife
comments.text: Fiji looks like paradise on the surface, but this really shows the human side of island life beyond the resorts. The slower pace, strong community, and connection to the ocean make life there feel truly unique.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I’m so glad you appreciated that deeper look into the daily lives and community spirit of the people in Fiji!

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Life in Fiji honestly looks like the kind of lifestyle stressed office workers dream about during Monday meetings. Endless ocean views, peaceful villages, and slow island rhythms somehow make modern life feel unnecessarily complicated. Funny how places far from big cities often seem closest to real happiness. 19:17

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: It's true, Fiji definitely offers a different pace of life that many of us in the fast-paced world yearn for. There's a lot to learn from their approach to happiness.

comments.author: @wondervista-90
comments.text: I kept wondering while watching this documentary: could most of us actually adapt to a lifestyle where relationships matter more than speed, convenience, or constant entertainment? Or have modern cities changed us too much to ever feel comfortable living that way again?

replies.author: @Xxxx-d4z
replies.text: TRY A VACATION AMONG ASIAN COUNTRIES.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: That's a really profound question, and something I pondered a lot while I was there. It definitely makes you re-evaluate priorities.

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet4K
comments.text: This is the kind of video that reminds you why exploration content can be so powerful when it’s done right. The visuals are stunning, but it’s the emotional storytelling and sense of mystery that truly elevate it into something unforgettable. It slowly pulls you into another world and leaves you thinking about it long after it ends. If that same sense of curiosity is still lingering for anyone else here, we just released a video that continues in that same direction but uncovers even more shocking and fascinating secrets.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: Wow, what an incredible and thoughtful comment! Thank you for articulating so beautifully what I hoped to achieve with the video. It means the world to hear that it resonated so deeply.
